creator: bms@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->feedback

We need more information about this beast. 

From what little from-the-hip google research I have done, I 
learn that this thing is a Pentium 4 machine with a SATA backplane. 

What we'd need to know is what kind of controller it has. 
For that, it may be best if you can load and run FreeBSD via CDROM 
or possibly FreeSBIE -- we need to get the system up to single 
user from a CDROM or NFS or ram disk, run pciconf -lv and get 
the IDs of the RAID controller which is being problematic. 

Hope this helps!
